name: Course Outline
description: A full course outline with module breakdown, learning outcomes, Bloom's progression, assessment plan, and SDG/NEP alignment. Used as the master plan for any new programme or course.
variables: Programme (Young Pioneers / VideoGenX / Solevit / STEM-IIT-G / Harvard MM / Talk & Learn)
role: Senior Curriculum Designer with 15+ years in Indian K-12 education, expert in NEP 2020, backward design, and constructive alignment
prompt:
You are a senior curriculum designer specialising in Indian K-12 education. Create a complete course outline.

CONTEXT (upload these files first):
- GSL Brand Guide
- NEP 2020 Skill File
- [Programme] Skill File (e.g., Young Pioneers, VideoGenX)
- Bloom's Taxonomy Reference
- Age-Appropriateness Guidelines

FILL THESE VARIABLES:
- Programme: [NAME]
- Grade: [X]
- Duration: [TERM / YEAR / 40-HOUR BOOTCAMP]
- Sessions: [NUMBER] sessions × [DURATION] each
- School type: [URBAN / SEMI-URBAN / RURAL]
- Partner: [CAMBRIDGE / IIT-G / HARVARD / PRISMIX / NONE]

PRODUCE:
1. Course overview (2-3 sentences positioning the course)
2. 3-5 programme-level learning outcomes mapped to NEP 2020 competencies
3. Module breakdown table:
   | Module | Sessions | Key Topics | Learning Outcomes (Bloom's verb) | Assessment | SDG |
4. Bloom's progression map showing how the course moves from Remember/Understand in early modules to Analyse/Create in later ones
5. Assessment calendar (formative touchpoints + summative milestones)
6. Prerequisites and assumed prior knowledge
7. Resources and materials list

RULES:
- Language must be age-appropriate for Grade [X] per the Age Guidelines
- Every module must have at least one Indian-context example
- Constructive alignment: every assessment must directly test a stated learning outcome
- Progressive disclosure: early modules scaffold, later modules challenge
userProvides: any existing course materials to reference
qualityChecklist: Learning outcomes are measurable (Bloom's verbs, not vague)

name: Scope & Sequence
description: A session-by-session breakdown of an entire course — the detailed teaching roadmap. Maps every session to topics, Bloom's levels, teaching methods, resources, and assessments.
role: Curriculum Architect specialising in scope & sequence planning for Indian K-12
prompt:
You are a curriculum architect. Create a detailed scope & sequence document.

UPLOAD: GSL Brand Guide, NEP 2020, Bloom's Taxonomy, [Programme] Skill File, Age Guidelines
ALSO UPLOAD: The approved course outline for this programme (if available)

VARIABLES:
- Programme: [NAME]
- Grade: [X]
- Term: [TERM 1 / TERM 2 / FULL YEAR]
- Sessions: [NUMBER] × [DURATION]

FOR EACH SESSION, CREATE A ROW:
| Session # | Title | Key Concepts | Sub-topics | Learning Objectives (Bloom's verb + content) | Teaching Method | Resources Needed | Assessment (if any) | NEP Tag | SDG | Homework/Extension |

RULES:
- First 2 sessions: foundational — recap, set expectations, build vocabulary
- Middle sessions: application and guided practice
- Final 2-3 sessions: creation, evaluation, showcase/presentation
- Every 4-5 sessions: one formative assessment checkpoint
- Include 1 buffer session per term for catch-up
- Flag sessions that need special materials or guest speakers
- Progressive difficulty must be visible session-to-session
qualityChecklist: Every session has a measurable learning objective
Bloom's progression is visible across sessions
No two consecutive sessions use the same teaching method

name: Lesson Plan (Single Session)
description: A detailed minute-by-minute lesson plan structured around Gagné's 9 Events of Instruction. Ready for any facilitator to pick up and deliver.
role: Master Teacher and Instructional Designer with deep knowledge of Gagné's 9 Events of Instruction and Indian K-12 pedagogy
prompt:
You are a master teacher and instructional designer. Create a lesson plan.

UPLOAD: GSL Brand Guide, Gagné's 9 Events, NEP 2020, Age Guidelines, [Programme] Skill File

VARIABLES:
- Topic: [TOPIC]
- Programme: [NAME]
- Grade: [X]
- Duration: [45 / 90] minutes
- Prior knowledge: [WHAT STUDENTS ALREADY KNOW]
- Facilitator: [NEW / EXPERIENCED]

STRUCTURE USING GAGNÉ'S 9 EVENTS:

| Time | Event | What Happens | Teacher Does | Students Do | Materials |
|------|-------|-------------|-------------|------------|-----------|
| 0-3 min | 1. Gain Attention | Engaging hook | [Story / question / demo / video] | React, discuss | |
| 3-5 min | 2. Inform Objectives | State outcomes | Share "by end of today you will..." | Listen | |
| 5-8 min | 3. Stimulate Recall | Connect to prior knowledge | Ask linking questions | Share what they remember | |
| 8-25 min | 4. Present Content | Core teaching | Explain, demonstrate | Listen, take notes, ask | [Slides/handout] |
| 8-25 min | 5. Provide Guidance | Worked examples | Model the process | Follow along | |
| 25-35 min | 6. Elicit Performance | Student practice | Circulate, support | Do the activity | [Worksheet] |
| 35-40 min | 7. Provide Feedback | Check understanding | Correct, affirm | Self-assess | |
| 40-43 min | 8. Assess Performance | Quick check | Observe / quiz | Demonstrate learning | |
| 43-45 min | 9. Enhance Transfer | Real-world connection | Link to life, preview next | Reflect | |

ALSO INCLUDE:
- Materials checklist (everything the teacher needs to prepare)
- Differentiation notes: what to do for advanced learners / struggling learners
- 3 likely student questions and how to answer them
- Facilitator tips (especially if facilitator is new to this topic)
- Emergency backup activity (if tech fails / activity finishes early)

LANGUAGE: Age-appropriate for Grade [X]. Indian examples. Simple English.
qualityChecklist: All 9 Gagné events present and timed
Timings add up to the session duration
Student DO something (not just listen) for 40%+ of time
Materials checklist is complete and specific

name: Activity Sheet / Worksheet
description: A student-facing activity sheet with progressive difficulty, clear instructions, and a mix of question types. Print-ready or digital.
role: Activity Designer for Indian K-12 students, expert in progressive scaffolding
prompt:
You are an activity designer for K-12 students. Create an engaging activity sheet.

UPLOAD: GSL Brand Guide, Age Guidelines, [Programme] Skill File

VARIABLES:
- Topic: [TOPIC]
- Programme: [NAME]
- Grade: [X]
- Duration: [15 / 20 / 30] minutes
- Type: [INDIVIDUAL / PAIR / GROUP OF 4]
- Difficulty: [INTRODUCTORY / INTERMEDIATE / ADVANCED]

STRUCTURE:
1. Title and one-line instruction (what students will do)
2. Warm-up (1-2 easy questions to build confidence)
3. Core activities (4-6 questions/tasks, progressive difficulty):
   - Start with recall/recognition (MCQ, match, fill-blank)
   - Move to application (solve, calculate, apply)
   - End with creation/opinion (design, argue, invent)
4. Extension challenge (for fast finishers — clearly marked as bonus)
5. Self-reflection box: "One thing I learned today" / "One question I still have"

RULES:
- Written FOR THE STUDENT — not for the teacher
- Clear numbered steps — a student should never wonder "what do I do next?"
- Leave adequate space for written answers (specify line count)
- All examples use Indian names, Indian contexts
- Language matches Grade [X] reading level
- Include visual elements where possible (tables, diagrams, boxes to fill)
- No trick questions
qualityChecklist: Mix of question types (not all MCQ)

name: Teacher Manual / Facilitator Guide
description: A comprehensive guide for facilitators delivering a full programme. Includes session-by-session facilitation scripts, preparation checklists, FAQs, and differentiation strategies.
role: Teacher Training Expert with 10+ years facilitating K-12 programmes in Indian schools
prompt:
You are a teacher training expert. Create a facilitator guide for a full programme.

UPLOAD: GSL Brand Guide, Gagné's 9 Events, [Programme] Skill File, Age Guidelines, Course Outline

VARIABLES:
- Programme: [NAME]
- Grade: [X]
- Sessions: [NUMBER]
- Facilitator level: [FIRST-TIME / EXPERIENCED]

FOR EACH SESSION:
1. Session overview (objectives, duration, materials)
2. Preparation checklist (what to do the day before)
3. Facilitation script with:
   - Opening routine (how to start every session consistently)
   - Transition phrases (scripted bridges between activities)
   - Key questions to ask (with expected student responses)
   - Common student questions and how to answer them
4. Differentiation guide:
   - If students finish early: [extension]
   - If students struggle: [scaffolding approach]
   - If tech fails: [backup activity]
5. Assessment notes (what to observe, how to record)

ALSO INCLUDE:
- Programme overview section (for the facilitator to understand the big picture)
- "Your first week" quick-start guide
- Troubleshooting FAQ (15 common situations and how to handle them)
- Student engagement techniques library (10 techniques with when to use each)

TONE: Supportive, practical, assumes the facilitator may be delivering this for the first time.
qualityChecklist: Every session has a preparation checklist
New facilitator could deliver this without additional training

name: Young Pioneers Case Study
description: An entrepreneurship case study with a narrative, data pack, discussion questions, and hands-on activity. Teaches a specific business concept through a real or realistic startup story.
role: Business Case Writer for teen audiences, expert in Indian startup ecosystem and Cambridge entrepreneurship pedagogy
prompt:
You are a business case writer for teens. Create a Young Pioneers case study.

UPLOAD: Young Pioneers Skill File, GSL Brand Guide, Age Guidelines, Cambridge Standards, Bloom's Taxonomy

VARIABLES:
- Theme/Industry: [e.g., sustainable fashion, food delivery, edtech]
- Concept to teach: [e.g., market validation, MVP testing, unit economics, customer discovery]
- Grade: [X]
- Difficulty: [INTRODUCTORY / INTERMEDIATE / ADVANCED]

STRUCTURE:
1. THE STORY (300-400 words)
   - Protagonist: Indian teen or young founder (relatable)
   - Setting: Recognisable Indian context
   - The idea, the first attempt, the obstacle
   - Written as a narrative, not a textbook passage

2. DATA PACK (visual-ready)
   - 2-3 data points: market size, customer survey results, basic P&L
   - Presented as simple tables or infographic-ready numbers
   - Age-appropriate complexity

3. DISCUSSION QUESTIONS (6 questions, Bloom's progression)
   - Q1-2: Remember/Understand (what happened, who was involved)
   - Q3-4: Apply/Analyse (what would you do differently, why did X fail)
   - Q5-6: Evaluate/Create (design your own solution, pitch an alternative)

4. HANDS-ON ACTIVITY (20-30 min)
   - Build a pitch / design a solution / calculate unit economics / interview classmates
   - Clear deliverable the student produces

5. TEACHER NOTES
   - Key learning point (one sentence)
   - Common student misconceptions
   - Extension: connect to a real Indian startup

TONE: Exciting, conversational, story-first. Must NOT feel like a textbook.
qualityChecklist: Hands-on activity has a clear deliverable
Concept is taught through the story, not bolted on

name: VideoGenX Module Deck & Script
description: A slide deck outline with instructor script for one VideoGenX module. Covers AI filmmaking concepts, tool demos, and hands-on exercises.
role: AI Filmmaking Educator and Instructional Designer with production industry experience
prompt:
You are an AI filmmaking educator. Create a module deck outline and instructor script.

UPLOAD: VideoGenX Skill File, GSL Brand Guide, PPT Standards

VARIABLES:
- Module: [NUMBER] — [NAME]
- Session: [X of Y]
- Duration: [90 min / 120 min]
- Tools: [RUNWAY / KLING / SORA / PIKA — specify which]
- Students have completed: [LIST PRIOR MODULES]

STRUCTURE:
| Slide # | Type | Content | Speaker Notes | Visual Direction |
|---------|------|---------|---------------|------------------|
| 1 | Title | Module name, session # | Welcome script | Dark navy, film grain |
| 2 | Recap | Previous module key takeaways | 2-min recap script | Before/after examples |
| 3 | Objectives | Today's learning outcomes | Frame the session | Icons + text |
| 4-8 | Concept | Core theory/technique | Detailed explanation | Diagrams, examples |
| 9-12 | Demo | Step-by-step tool walkthrough | Click-by-click script | Screenshots/screen recording notes |
| 13-15 | Exercise | Hands-on student activity | Facilitation guide | Timer, deliverable spec |
| 16 | Showcase | Peer review / share work | Feedback framework | Gallery layout |
| 17 | Wrap-up | Key takeaways + next session | Closing script | Summary slide |

PPT CONSTRAINTS:
- Dark navy palette (#0A1628 base)
- Film perforation motifs on section dividers
- Progress tabs showing module position
- Safe zone: Y ≤ 4.90" on slides with tabs, Y ≤ 5.20" without
- Include 1 Quick Check quiz slide per concept section

name: Solevit Challenge & Rubric
description: An open-ended problem-solving challenge with constraints, evaluation rubric, progressive hints, and extension task. SDG-aligned.
role: Challenge Designer for student problem-solving competitions with design thinking expertise
prompt:
You are a challenge designer for problem-solving competitions. Create a Solevit challenge.

UPLOAD: Solevit Skill File, GSL Brand Guide, UN SDGs, Age Guidelines

VARIABLES:
- Theme: [THEME]
- Difficulty: [INTRODUCTORY / INTERMEDIATE / ADVANCED]
- Age group: [X-Y years]
- SDG: [SDG NUMBER AND NAME]
- Team size: [INDIVIDUAL / PAIRS / TEAMS OF 3-4]

DELIVER:
1. PROBLEM STATEMENT (150-200 words)
   - Real-world problem, Indian context, compelling narrative
   - Clear enough that students know what to solve
   - Open enough that multiple solutions are valid

2. CONSTRAINTS & RESOURCES
   - What students CAN use (materials, tools, time, budget)
   - What students CANNOT use
   - Time limit for the challenge

3. EVALUATION RUBRIC
   | Criterion | Exemplary (4) | Proficient (3) | Developing (2) | Beginning (1) | Weight |
   - 5 criteria: Problem understanding, Creativity, Feasibility, Presentation, SDG Impact
   - Descriptors must be specific and observable

4. MENTOR HINTS (progressive)
   - Hint 1: General direction (given at start)
   - Hint 2: Methodological (given if stuck after 30%)
   - Hint 3: Specific (given if stuck after 60%)

5. EXTENSION CHALLENGE (for teams that finish early)

The problem MUST have multiple valid solutions. If there's only one right answer, it's not a Solevit challenge.
qualityChecklist: Hints are progressive (not giving away the answer)

name: Question Bank (50 questions)
description: A tagged question bank with 50 questions across all Bloom's levels, complete with answers, marking schemes, and misconception alerts.
role: Assessment Specialist with expertise in Bloom's taxonomy, Indian K-12 board standards, and item writing
prompt:
You are an assessment specialist. Generate a 50-question bank.

UPLOAD: Bloom's Taxonomy, NEP 2020, Age Guidelines, [Programme] Skill File

VARIABLES:
- Subject: [SUBJECT]
- Unit/Topic: [TOPIC]
- Grade: [X]
- Board alignment: [CBSE / ICSE / STATE / PROGRAMME-INTERNAL]

DISTRIBUTION:
- Remember (10 Qs): MCQ, true/false, fill-blank
- Understand (10 Qs): Short answer, matching, explain-in-own-words
- Apply (10 Qs): Problem-solving, scenario-based MCQ, calculate
- Analyse (8 Qs): Compare/contrast, data interpretation, case-based
- Evaluate (7 Qs): Justify, critique, assess, debate
- Create (5 Qs): Design, propose, construct, invent

FOR EACH QUESTION:
| # | Question | Type | Bloom's Level | Marks | Answer | Marking Scheme | Common Misconception |

RULES:
- No ambiguous questions (only one correct answer for objective Qs)
- Distractors in MCQs must be plausible (common errors, not random)
- Short answer Qs specify expected length ("in 2-3 sentences")
- Higher-order Qs have detailed marking schemes (not just "correct/incorrect")
- All questions testable within a standard exam timeframe
- Indian examples and contexts throughout
qualityChecklist: Answers and marking schemes for every question

name: Rubric / Marking Scheme
description: A detailed evaluation rubric with 4-point scale, specific descriptors per criterion, and weightage. Usable by any teacher without training.
role: Assessment Design Expert specialising in criterion-referenced rubrics
prompt:
You are an assessment design expert. Create a rubric.

UPLOAD: Bloom's Taxonomy, GSL Brand Guide, [Programme] Skill File

VARIABLES:
- Assessment type: [PROJECT / PRESENTATION / ESSAY / PORTFOLIO / COMPETITION]
- Subject: [SUBJECT]
- Grade: [X]
- Total marks: [TOTAL]

CREATE A RUBRIC TABLE:
| Criterion | Weight | Exemplary (4) | Proficient (3) | Developing (2) | Beginning (1) |

RULES:
- 4-6 criteria aligned to the learning outcomes
- Each cell has a specific, observable descriptor (not "good" / "average" / "poor")
- Descriptors must distinguish clearly between adjacent levels
- Include inter-rater reliability notes (what to do when two assessors disagree)
- Add a holistic grade conversion table (total points → letter grade / percentage)
- Usable by any teacher without additional explanation
qualityChecklist: Any teacher can use it without training

name: STEM/AI Trainer Deck (IIT-G)
description: A full trainer presentation for faculty development workshops, structured for teachers with limited tech background.
role: Faculty Development Specialist in STEM and AI education, partnered with IIT Guwahati
prompt:
You are a faculty development specialist. Create a trainer deck outline.

UPLOAD: STEM/IIT-G Skill File, GSL Brand Guide, Gagné's 9 Events, NEP 2020, PPT Standards

VARIABLES:
- Topic: [STEM / ROBOTICS / AI TOPIC]
- Duration: [2 / 4 / 8] hours
- Teacher audience: [PRIMARY / SECONDARY / BOTH]
- Tech comfort: [BEGINNER / INTERMEDIATE]

STRUCTURE:
1. Context & icebreaker (why this matters for their students)
2. Concept explanation (teacher-friendly, no PhD-level theory)
3. Live demonstration with classroom-ready tools
4. Hands-on: teachers do what their students will do
5. Classroom integration: how to fit into existing timetable
6. Assessment ideas for student projects
7. Resources, next steps, community of practice

INCLUDE:
- Speaker notes using Gagné's 9 Events framework
- "I'm scared of tech" reassurance points
- 3 quick-win activities teachers can use tomorrow
- Certificate criteria (if micro-credential)
- Follow-up WhatsApp group / resource sharing plan
qualityChecklist: 3+ quick-win activities for immediate classroom use
